description: Displays the name of the current directory. (In CMD, using cd without parameters prints the path).
Creates a directory tree. CMD automatically creates parent directories by default.
Removes a directory and all files and subdirectories within it, in quiet mode.

flags.meaning: Removes all directories and files in the specified directory in addition to the directory itself.
Quiet mode, do not ask if ok to remove a directory tree with /s.

description: Searches for a file in the current directory and all subdirectories.

flags.meaning: Displays files in specified directory and all subdirectories.
Uses bare format (no heading information or summary).

description: Displays the contents of a text file on the screen.
Displays a list of currently running processes with verbose information (does not auto-refresh).

flags.meaning: Specifies that verbose task information be displayed in the output.

description: Displays a list of currently running processes on either a local or remote machine.
Forcefully ends one or more tasks or processes using the Process ID.

flags.meaning: Specifies the process ID of the task to be terminated.
Forcefully terminates the process.

description: Forcefully terminates a process using its image (executable) name.

flags.meaning: Specifies the image name of the process to be terminated.
Forcefully terminates the process.

description: Starts an application or script without opening a new Command Prompt window.

flags.meaning: Starts the application without opening a new window. CTRL+C will be ignored.

description: Creates a new local user account with a specified password.

flags.meaning: Adds a user account to the local user accounts database.

description: Prompts the administrator to securely type a new password for the specified user.

flags.meaning: Produces a prompt for the password so it is not visible on the screen when typed.

description: Opens a new Command Prompt window running under the context of a different user.

flags.meaning: Specifies the user account to use to run the program.

description: Displays a list of all user accounts configured on the computer.
Permanently deletes a local user account from the system.

flags.meaning: Removes the specified user account.

description: Displays detailed configuration information about the computer and its operating system, including hardware properties and installed updates.
Queries the Windows Management Instrumentation to display the total size and free space of all connected drives.

flags.meaning: Specifies exactly which properties to retrieve from the WMI logical disk query.

description: Pipes the output of systeminfo into the find command to specifically isolate and display the exact date and time the system last booted.

flags.meaning: Pipes the output of the first command into the second command.

description: Forces the computer to immediately shut down and restart.

flags.meaning: Full shutdown and restart of the computer.
Sets the time-out period before shutdown to zero seconds (immediate).

description: Starts a registered Windows network or system service from the command line.
Modifies the Access Control List (ACL) of a file or folder to grant specific permissions to a user.

flags.meaning: Grants specified user access rights. Permissions replace previously granted explicit permissions.
Grants Full Control access rights to the specified user.

description: Enables an administrator to recover access to a file that was denied by re-assigning file ownership to the current user.

flags.meaning: Specifies the file name or directory name pattern to take ownership of.

description: Executes the Command Prompt under the context of the local Administrator account (prompts for password).

flags.meaning: Specifies the exact administrative account to run the process as.

description: Displays the user, group, and privileges information for the user who is currently logged on to the local system.

flags.meaning: Displays the current user name, group names, security identifiers (SID), and privileges.

description: Generates a new SSH key pair using Windows' native OpenSSH implementation for secure remote authentication.

flags.meaning: Specifies the modern, highly secure Ed25519 cryptographic algorithm.
Adds a descriptive comment to the public key file.

description: Verifies IP-level connectivity to another TCP/IP computer by sending Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) echo requests. Defaults to 4 requests.
Displays the full TCP/IP configuration for all network adapters, including DNS, MAC addresses, and IPv4/IPv6 details.

flags.meaning: Displays the full configuration information. Without this, ipconfig displays only basic IP, subnet, and gateway data.

description: Determines the path taken to a destination by sending ICMP Echo Request messages with incrementally increasing Time to Live (TTL) field values.
Displays active TCP connections, ports on which the computer is listening, Ethernet statistics, and the PID of the process tying up the port.

flags.meaning: Displays all active connections and the TCP and UDP ports on which the computer is listening.
Displays active TCP connections, but addresses and port numbers are expressed numerically.
Displays active TCP connections and includes the process ID (PID) for each connection.

description: Displays information that you can use to diagnose Domain Name System (DNS) infrastructure.
Displays the state of the working directory and the staging area in a Git repository.
Tests an HTTP endpoint. Native curl is available in Windows 10 build 17063 and later.

flags.meaning: Include the HTTP response headers in the output.

description: Searches for patterns of text in files, commonly used for parsing log files for errors.

flags.meaning: Searches for matching files in the current directory and all subdirectories.
Specifies that the search is not to be case-sensitive.
Prints the line number before each line that matches.

description: Extracts the contents of a zip archive. Native tar is available in Windows 10 build 17063 and later.

flags.meaning: Extract files from an archive.
Use archive file or device ARCHIVE.

description: Displays, sets, or removes cmd.exe environment variables.
Clears the command prompt window and screen buffer.
Displays all commands stored in memory for the current command prompt session.

flags.meaning: Outputs the list of previously typed commands.

description: Downloads a file using the native curl executable (available in modern Windows 10/11 CMD).

flags.meaning: Writes the output to the specified file instead of standard output.

description: Provides help information and syntax documentation for Windows commands.
Graphically displays the folder structure of a drive or path.

flags.meaning: Displays the names of the files in each folder in addition to the folder structure.

description: Finds the Process ID (PID) listening on a specific network port by piping netstat output into the findstr tool.

flags.meaning: Displays all active connections and listening ports.
Displays addresses and port numbers in numerical form.
Displays the owning process ID associated with each connection.

description: Prints the current system date and time to the console without prompting the user to enter a new one.
Creates a temporary macro/shortcut for a command in the current CMD session.
CMD lacks a native visual calendar grid, so this invokes PowerShell to print a formatted current date and time.

flags.meaning: Executes the specified string as a command and then instantly exits the PowerShell session.

description: Counts the number of lines in a file by searching for all lines that do not contain an empty string.

flags.meaning: Displays only the count of lines containing the string.
Displays all lines NOT containing the specified string.

description: Creates an infinite loop in CMD to clear the screen, run a command, and wait 2 seconds, simulating the UNIX 'watch' tool.

flags.meaning: Pauses the execution of the loop for 2 seconds before repeating.

description: CMD lacks a strong native crypto generator, so this invokes PowerShell to generate a secure random GUID string.

flags.meaning: Executes the specified commands as though they were typed at the PowerShell prompt.

description: Flushes and resets the contents of the DNS client resolver cache. Essential for fixing local network mapping issues.

flags.meaning: The specific flag that purges the DNS cache.

description: Uses the native Windows Certificate Utility to encode the contents of a text file into Base64 format.

flags.meaning: Encodes a file to Base64 format.

description: Prints the network routing table, specifically filtered to show the default gateway route.

flags.meaning: Tells the route command to display the routing table.
Filters the print command to only show the default network destination.

description: Displays a table listing all local network interfaces and their administrative and connection states.
Installs a software application using the Windows Package Manager.
Searches the Windows Package Manager repository for software.
